description: In a sleepy town in China, a middle-aged man is unwilling to endure a stagnant existence, and experiencing true love, decides to come out of the closet. Meanwhile, a lesbian couple go in search of a gay man, in order to get married and have a child. Behind these fiery love stories lies an absurd drama about identity politics, as these lonely souls struggle with the legacy of the communist past. Zhang Zhiyong gives a superb performance in the lead role and won Best Actor at the Golden Horse Awards, where the film was nominated in all the major categories; it also won best cinematography and editing. Stylising daily reality with a biting black humour, director Geng Jun subtly critiques popular assumptions about gender roles, social etiquette, and what is considered age-appropriate behaviour.
name: Queer East: Bel Ami (18)

description:
Dir: Sandro David Arceo Espinosa

A deeply human story about survival, desperation, and the things we cling to when everything falls apart.
In a world suffocating under water and medicine shortages, inventor Lucio is pouring everything he has into fixing his medical robot. It's his last hope. But as the machine sparks and stutters, so does his grip on what matters most.
Gritty, claustrophobic, and impossible to shake, Beings asks a simple but devastating question: what happens to our humanity when our tools — and our empathy — start to break down?

Duration: 103mins
name: Sci-Fi London 2026: Being (18)

description:
Director: Lloyd Eyre-Morgan and Neil Ely

Duration: 78mins

Jordan is a 30-something facing terminal cancer in 2027, drifting through morphine dreams and digital distractions. On a whim, he fires up an old Chatroulette-style app — and meets Danny, sarcastic, vibrant, and achingly alive. The catch? Danny swears it's 2005, and he's dying too.
Anchored by Siobhan Finneran (Happy Valley) and a raw, heartbreaking lead duo, Chatlines weaves queer romance, Northern grit, and time-bending tenderness into something that stays with you long after the screen goes dark. A film about loneliness, longing, and the desperate, beautiful search for connection when time is running out.
name: Sci-Fi London 2026: Chatlines (18)

description:
Director: Steven Lee Taylor

Duration: 80mins 

The world is experiencing temporal anomalies, and when Priscilla's son is suddenly ripped from her life, grief nearly destroys her. Then comes an offer she can't refuse: go back. Stop the man responsible before he ever invents the technology that unravels time itself.
Thrust back into her 21-year-old body, Priscilla has only four hours on a sun-drenched college hiking trail to complete her mission. Her target hasn't committed any crime yet, but she has to hunt him anyway.
name: Sci-Fi London 2026: Relive (18)

description:
Director: Luke Spears

Duration: 80mins

In a dystopian tomorrow, parole has been replaced by R.A.P.P. — the Recidivism Accountability Partnership Program — and survival now means forced collaboration. A white-collar criminal is tethered to a sociopathic gangster with terminal cancer. Both men carry explosive charges surgically implanted in their hearts. If either falters, both die.
Director Luke Spears builds a relentless pressure cooker from this simple, brutal premise — two men who despise each other, bound together by the leanest life insurance policy imaginable. Shackled is a lean, heart-pounding thriller where freedom is a shared sentence, and trust is the most dangerous gamble of all.
name: Sci-Fi London 2026: Shackled (18)

description:
Director: Jonathan Sobol.

Starring: Isabelle Fuhrman, Josh Hutcherson, Dennis Quaid, and David Thewlis

Duration: 88mins 

When tech billionaire Sam Houston recruits brilliant computer scientist Annika to work at a secretive facility, she stumbles into a discovery that changes everything: alien intelligences are communicating all around us, right now. But when the mission shifts from listening to talking back, the protocol fractures. The only problem? We may be far too primitive to understand what they're saying — and with contact comes consequences, with the survival of our species suddenly very much on the line.
name: Sci-Fi London 2026: Signal One (18)

description:
Director: Xiang Chen

Duration: 93mins

Chinese (English subs)

In Xiang Chen's breath taking debut, the apocalypse arrives not with a bang but an upload. The world's population has migrated its consciousness into a vast digital New World, leaving physical Earth eerily, beautifully abandoned. Sixteen-year-old Cheng Qi is the only one who won't log in — and so he walks, alone, through the haunting ruins of a deserted China.
The empty mega-cities here aren't just backdrop — they're the beating heart of the film. Chen uses vast, silent wide shots to make the case that something irreplaceable has been lost. As Cheng Qi pushes forward, even the frame itself begins to shrink around him, until he steps beyond its edges entirely — a boy too real for the world that remains.
name: Sci-Fi London 2026: The Journey To No End (18)

description:
Director: Sebastian Bărădău’,  

Duration: 90mins

Romanian with English subtitles

Sebastian Bărădău's debut feature is a razor-sharp, claustrophobic sci-fi that trades CGI spectacle for something far more exhilarating — the raw, electric tension of three scientists on the brink of a quantum-mechanics breakthrough that could redefine reality itself.
But discovery is never clean. As the evidence mounts, so does the conflict: bitter, personal, and suffocatingly human. The Uncertainty Principle is hypnotic, intellectually fearless, and shot with a gritty intensity that makes every argument feel like a fistfight. Like the science it's built on, the film offers no easy answers — just a transfixing puzzle that lingers long after the credits roll.
name: Sci-Fi London 2026: The Uncertainty Principle (18)

description:
Director: Marianna Dean

Duration: 87mins

Starring Zoe Cunningham, James Cosmo (Game of Thrones), Mim Shaikh, and Neil Bishop

To join the elite ATIC Guard, Alana Toro must pass one final test: enter a simulation, prevent a terrorist attack, and prove her loyalty. Every mistake resets the clock. Every death disappears — except in her memory, where the weight keeps building.
The more Alana learns, the more she begins to question the organisation she fought so hard to join. The enemies she's been sent to stop start to look less like threats. The system that's testing her starts to feel less like a tool and more like a trap.
Voidance is a taut, intelligent British sci-fi thriller about the cost of obedience — and what happens when the system resets, but you don't.
name: Sci-Fi London 2026: Voidance (18)

description:
Director: Sam Voutas

Duration: 91mins 

When failed novelist Amos agrees to mind a friend's remote research station for just one day, he expects solitude and a chance to break his writer's block. What he gets is something far stranger — on Yesterday Island, one day turns out to be a terrifyingly elastic concept.
Desperate to escape, Amos coaxes his ex-wife onto the island, only to strand them both there. To win their freedom, the two must scheme to invite their most disliked friends along. A comedy of errors where the errors are fatal.
Inspired by Greek myth and the trickery of the Labours of Hercules, Sam Voutas' award-winning dark comedy is bewildering, astonishing, and impossible to forget.
name: Sci-Fi London 2026: Yesterday Island (18)

description:
Director: Stillz
Date & Time: Monday, June 1st, 9.00 - 10.34 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 3
Blurb: Visionary music director Stillz makes his debut in this violent, raw and hunting 90s-set found footage. 

Sitting somewhere between documentary, fiction and nightmare, Barrio Triste is the unclassifiable film debut of visionary music director Stillz (who’s made videos for Bad Bunny, Rosalía, and Omar Apollo). Set in the outskirts of Medellín, Colombia, in the ’90s, the premise is deceptively simple: a camera falls into the hands of a group of lawless young men, who use it to document the everyday violence, criminality, and lovelessness they have become used to. Violent, raw, and sad, it’s a film of bold creative swings and extreme emotionality. Stillz is clearly a singular, original voice, and it shows in every frame of Barrio Triste. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026 Barrio Triste (18)

description:
Director: Various
Date & Time: Wednesday, June 3rd, 3.45 - 5.43 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: Fateful meetings and clandestine confrontations. Short films depicting the ways in which we cross paths.

From roads, rodeos, saunas, trains or pools, our lives are populated by small, brief encounters in unlikely places. From the thralls of a dark Western obsession, to the self-examinings of a cyber cowboy. Waiting for a hunk to scoop us up from the bottom of a public pool, or realising the worst about your partner after being trapped in a private sauna. Our encounters with God, the Other, the unexplained, whether in the dense Cuban rainforest or a crowded French train. BRIEF ENCOUNTERS brings together the best in contemporary short films, asking a simple question: who might we meet next?
Total Running Time: 88 min
name: SXSW 2026 Shorts: Brief Encounters + Q&A (18)

description:
Director: Various
Date & Time: Friday, June 5th, 6.15 - 8.19 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: The soft murmur of distant voices. The hauntological and oblique, our personal lost utopias, memories we just can’t shake.

Seven films that chart our memories, realities, and objects of nostalgia: The last remaining Mimic in England, impersonating those we have lost; Bubba, a virtual browser-dog snuffed out with new programming; a student who finds himself waking in the strangest of places; a mythical Mayan figure that brings death and despair; a shadow puppeteer haunted by memories of the Khmer Rouge; a man walking through island streets, searching for ghosts; a simple game of trading cards, taking place in the space between then and now. DISTANT VOICES investigates the hauntological, those lost things we can never get back.

Total Running Time: 94 min
name: SXSW 2026 Shorts: Distant Voices + Q&A (18)

description:
PREVIOUSLY CALLED Midnight Movies
Director: Various
Date & Time: Monday, June 1st, 8:40 PM - 10:37 PM 
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: An eclectic collection of provocative and unhinged shorts that challenge, shock, and subvert expectations.

Slip into the alluring absurdity and unhinged energy of this year’s MIDNIGHT MOVIES. Featuring alternative storytelling from around the world, spanning the darkest of comedies, edge-of-your-seat thrillers, frenzied animations and erotica so twisted you’ll want to bleach your eyeballs. A healthy dose of ghosts, ghouls and trauma is guaranteed. Join the cult - we promise you’ll leave alive, shaken and stirred.
name: SXSW 2026 Shorts: Ghosts, Ghouls and Glitches + Q&A (18)

description:
Director: Various
Date & Time: Monday, June 1st, 6:20 - 8:25 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 3
Blurb: An exploration of human behaviour through the eyes of animals, landscapes, and our uneasy relationship with the natural world. 

The most complex creatures within the animal kingdom are examined through a new lens in HUMAN / NATURE, a programme dedicated to exploring human behaviour in all forms. This collection of anthropomorphic fables delve into our hidden desires, inner anxieties and emphasises our connection with the natural world. In the wild, children and friends go missing, never to be found, a maned wolf is displaced and searches for a home. Humans living near the sea face an environmental crisis as microplastics invade their ecosystem. Questions about gender shake the bedrock of an elderly Malaysian-Tamil couple and a young woman’s obsession grows.
name: SXSW 2026 Shorts: Human Nature + Q&A (18)

description:
Director: Various
Date & Time: Thursday, June 4th, 6.00 - 8.05 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 1
Blurb: Where control is constant, rebellion slips through, and frustration and truth push back against systems of control. 

Across (and below) streets, borders, and households, the films in PROTEST WILL NOT BE TOLERATED trace acts of quiet defiance under pressure; from whispered rumours and bureaucratic barriers, to forbidden voices and everyday risks, each story reveals how control is enforced, and how it’s subtly resisted. Moments of play, hunger, curiosity and connection become gestures of rebellion, whether against state power, social expectation, or inherited silence. In worlds where protest is not permitted, resistance takes other forms: intimate, absurd, fragile, and persistent. Here, protest is not as expected, instead everything hums with an uncontainable resistance.

Total Running Time: 96 min
name: SXSW 2026 Shorts: Protest Will Not Be Tolerated + Q&A (18)

description:
Director: Various
Date & Time: Tuesday, June 2nd, 8.30 - 10.31 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 3
Blurb: Urban flânerie featuring unfolding city lives, collective struggles, and fleeting intimacies amid the hustle and bustle.

TINY, LONELY FISH FLOATING IN DARK WATER brings together a mosaic of urban gazes across continents, weaving everyday situations and collective anecdotes into cinematic vignettes. From neon-lit nights in 1980s Tokyo to the fractured infrastructures of New York, from the rhythms of a Mexico City neighbourhood to the quiet vastness of Ulaanbaatar's empty high-rises, these films observe how people navigate urban space and each other. Together they orbit the many hearts of disparate cities, probing identities, intimacy, and resilience in search of forms of belonging that surface, fracture, and reconfigure within the shifting currents of urban life.
name: SXSW 2026 Shorts: Tiny, Lonely Fish Floating in Dark Water + Q&A (18)

description:
Director: Jason James
Date & Time: Tuesday, June 2nd, 8.40 - 10.45 PM 
RichMix Screen: Cinema 1
Blurb: Severance’s Zach Cherry and Borat’s Maria Bakalova go down a spiral of murder, lies, and (almost) sex in this riotous comedy.

On one fateful night, Gary (Zach Cherry) and Eli (Maria Bakalova) decide to take their brief dating app exchange into real life. When they meet at a bar, things move fast, and soon the duo is at a roadside motel ready for your regular one-night stand. But nothing about their night is regular as they find themselves driving the car of a possible killer, lugging a dead body, and running from the police while carrying a mysterious suitcase filled to the brim with dirty cash in this fast-paced, anxiety-inducing Canadian comedy. A regular one-night-stand, indeed. (RAFA SALES ROSS)
name: SXSW 2026: All Night Wrong (18)

description:
Director: Tim Travis Hawkins 
Date & Time: Friday, June 5th, 3.45 - 5.25 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 3
Blurb: Truth is way, way stranger than fiction in this twisty documentary about a lost zoo and an American family playing God with nature. 

Emmy-nominated documentarian Tim Travers Hawkins was hiking when he stumbled upon the ruins of a zoo in the middle of the woods. A set of lost home movies became the starting point of American Zoo, a documentary that blasts open the doors of the old animal ground to uncover three generations of family secrets that will take you from the sheltered forests of rural U.S. to the height of German fascism, investigating the viciousness of humans who experimented with genetic modifications in animals — viciously believing to be able to bend the living to their cruel will. A work of carefully concocted precision, Hawkins’s latest is best enjoyed without much prior knowledge of this twisty, almost unbelievable story. But trust us, you are in for one hell of a ride. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: American Zoo (18)

description:
Director: Siyou Tan
Date & Time: Thursday, June 4th, 4.00 - 6.18 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: The line between free-living and parasitic existence as a teenage girl is carefully walked in Siyou Tan’s debut feature film which explores the pitfalls of conformity and the consequences of going astray. 

16-year-old Choo Xin Yu (Ranice Tay) is the new student at an elite all girls school. Instead of doing her best to fit in and comply, Choo Xin Yu quickly forms a girl gang with three others.

The authoritarian backdrop of their competitive school becomes a symbol and reflection of their day to day existence in Singaporean society, with the school uniform being the only thing the young women comply with. Choo Xin Yu turns the camera on the world, the way she wants to see it. With glimpses of what the gang choose to document, through their eyes and perspective Tan creates a compelling tapestry of loyalty, righteousness and sisterhood, making the paths and broken bonds harder to stomach. 

Whether within the school walls, inside of a teenage girl’s bedroom or on  the streets of Singapore Amoeba explores coming-of-age under authoritarian rule, capturing the changing face of Singaporean identity and the desire to remain young while longing for real life to begin. (ISRA AL KASSI)
name: SXSW 2026: Amoeba (18)

description:
Director: Amirali Navaee
Date & Time: Wednesday, June 3rd, 8.45-11.13pm
RichMix Screen: Cinema 1
Blurb: In the remote town of Rajasthan, a revered leader is accused of sexual assault by an anonymous letter. 

Against her superiors’ orders, young officer Roohi (Huma Qureshi) takes it upon herself to get to the bottom of the case, unknowingly stumbling into a large web of corruption, community complacence and institutional enabling. Firmly rooted in the police procedural, Bayaan recalls the tenacity of Det. Oliva Benson from Law & Order: SVU, all while carving out a distinctively contemporary and regionally specific take on the familiar genre. Bayaan is a stong vehicle for Qureshi, who delivers a commanding performance as a woman fighting against both a system designed to protect abuses and the very women who help keep it in place. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: Bayaan + Q&A (18)

description:
Director: Zhannat Alshanova
Date & Time: Saturday, June 6th, 2.00 - 4.05 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 3
Blurb: A beautiful coming of age story where the act of rebellion is a search for stability amidst family chaos.

Teenage swimmer Mila takes care of the family home and her kid sister while her mother pursues affairs and opportunities. When Mila is recruited to the open water swimming team led by the stabilising and alluring Vlad,she is finally able to shine.. However,  in the aftermath of a teammate’s accident and her mother’s plans to move with a new paramour, Mila is faced with a new kind of fight: can rebelling also mean resisting adultification?  (ISRA AL KASSI)
name: SXSW 2026: Becoming

description:
Director: Aoqian Sun
Date & Time: Friday, June 5th, 1.00 - 3.16 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 3
Blurb: A crematorium worker in China mixes up two sets of ashes with deadly consequences 

Huang Yan (Song Jia) manages the furnace at a crematorium. On one fateful afternoon, she mixes two sets of ashes at work, with the families of the dead receiving wrongful remains. The woman believes the bad luck coming from the mix-up is causing the deadly illness ravaging her body and, on a desperate attempt to save her life, sets out to undo her mistake with the unexpected help of a young girl caught in the embroil. Aoqian Sun expertly traverses the lines between social drama and thriller to prod at questions of death and family within contemporary Chinese society. (RAFA SALES ROSS)
name: SXSW 2026: Embers (18)

description:
Director: Tereza Nvotová
Date & Time: Wednesday, June 3rd, 8:45 - 10.38 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 3
Blurb: How can society punish a man who’s lost everything?

It’s a big day for Michal (Milan Ondrík) as the new CEO of his company is coming to visit. It also happens to be one of the hottest days of the year. Michal heads to the office after dropping off his daughter at daycare. All of the above are in fact the gaps in memory and what unravels throughout Father is the dizzying depiction of one man’s life-changing fatal mistake. As Slovakia’s official Oscar submission of 2026 director Tereza Nvotová builds a world of overwhelming chaos with an idyllic family at the centre of it. Inspired by real life tragic events, Milan Ondrík delivers a career best performance as a man left with both impossible grief and an incomprehensible guilt. (ISRA AL KASSI)
name: SXSW 2026: Father (18)

description:
Director: Adrian Choa, Michael Boccalini
Date & Time: Monday, June 1st, 8:50 PM - 10:44 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 1
Blurb: East London’s buzzy Angelina restaurant chases their first Michelin star in this doc narrated by legendary chef Marco Pierre White.

There is a certain air of mystery around the famed Michelin stars sparsely given to the world’s best restaurants and chefs. Few people can explain the system as well as legendary chef Marco Pierre White, the first Brit to earn three of them — and the first to famously give them all back. The fascinating Feast or Famine lifts the heavy curtain of secrecy that surrounds high-end dining, with White demystifying the Michelin myth while the highly-regarded team behind East London’s buzzy Angelina restaurant chases their first ever coveted star. Watch it, and make your reservation. (RAFA SALES ROSS)
name: SXSW 2026: Feast or Famine (18)

description:
Director: Mohammed Sawwaf  
Date & Time: Tuesday, June 2nd, 3.30 - 5.36 PM 
RichMix Screen: Cinema 3 
Blurb: A mother fights to be reunited with her infant twins as the war in Gaza escalates  
 
As the war in Gaza escalates, Rania goes into high-risk labour with triplets. One girl dies at birth. Siblings Jowan and Hamoud survive, but acute health complications lead to a need for in-hospital care. When their hospital is evacuated, the infant twins are moved to southern Gaza, while Rania is trapped by travel bans in place in the north. Mohammed Sawwaf’s deeply moving doc chronicles a mother’s plight to be reunited with her children amongst the destruction of war, while also exposing the cruelty of withholding basic healthcare for families at times of unspeakable violence. (RAFA SALES ROSS)
name: SXSW 2026: Gaza Twins, Come Back To Me (18)

description:
Director: Shahad Ameen
Date & Time: Wednesday, June 3rd, 6:00 - 8.25 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 3
Blurb: A young girl and her grandmother have their pilgrimage to Mecca interrupted by a runaway in this multigenerational Saudi road movie.

A pilgrimage to Mecca is derailed when a devout grandmother (Khairiah Nathmy) loses one of her granddaughters and decides to embark on a road trip with remaining grandchild Janna (Lamar Faden) to find her before anyone else does. With the help of opportunistic driver Ahmed (Nawaf Al-Dhufairy), they make their way cross-country, letting the contradictions and unspoken tensions between them unfold. Director Shahad Ameen uses the landscape to draw parallels between the physical and emotional journeys of her characters (and, in a broader sense, the multiplicity of women in Saudi Arabia) so powerfully, you’ll feel the wind on your skin long after leaving the cinema. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: Hijra (18)

description:
Director: Elena Andreicheva
Date & Time: Tuesday, June 2nd, 3.45 - 5.28 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 1
Blurb: Leading global authorities join AI expert Marc Warner for a wargame exploring how artificial intelligence might reshape power.

Much has been said about the cataclysmic effect of artificial intelligence on fields such as technology and the creative arts, but we are just now beginning to experience the consequences of this new era on politics and its influence on geopolitical conflicts globally. Intelligence Rising chronicles a bold experiment: some of the world’s most prominent authorities in politics, military strategy, philosophy, and technology gather to play an intricate wargame designed by AI-expert-turned-agnostic Marc Warner. What ensues is a stark warning, but also a much-needed reminder of the still remaining power of human autonomy and empathy. (RAFA SALES ROSS)
name: SXSW 2026: Intelligence Rising (18)

description:
Director: Mohamed Jabarah Al-Daradji
Date & Time: Thursday, June 4th, 3.15 - 5.42 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 1
Blurb: A heartbreaking tale with glimpses of hope for the Baghdad children left behind after the war.  

In the labyrinthine streets of Baghdad, orphaned and homeless children roam aimlessly with nothing to rely on but each other. In a world of scarcity, some children like Moody (Hussein Raad Zuwayr) are lured by the empty promises of the armed militia, while others like nine-year-old dreamer Chum-Chum (Youssef Husham Al-Thahabi) find a rare sliver of hope in dreaming. Drawn to the Tigris River and the ancestral myth of Gilgamesh, Chum-Chum sets out on a journey to bring his parents back to life in this bittersweet epic from Mohamed Jabarah Al-Daradji, a heartbreaking but hopeful tale on the power of myth and memory. (ISRA AL KASSI)
name: SXSW 2026: Irkalla - Gilgamesh's Dream (18)

description:
Director: Mariana Rondón, Marité Ugás
Date & Time: Saturday, June 6th, 6.15 - 8.28 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: A woman must reconstruct her identity and sense of self in this gripping thriller set at the height of Venezuela’s political crisis.

Set against the chaos of Venezuela’s political crisis in 2017, this tense, propulsive thriller executive produced by Édgar Ramírez follows Adelaida (Natalia Reyes) as she navigates a Caracas where survival demands impossible choices. Returning from her mother’s funeral, she finds her home is seized by a violent female militia. Ousted from her own house and surrounded by a city of escalating tensions and collapsing institutions, Adelaida discovers that her neighbour has just passed away – and left her Spanish passport behind. Set during the few tension-filled days that follow, It Would Be Night in Caracas deftly navigates the morality of survival, as Adelaida navigates a corrupt system, a lawless environment and her own morals to ensure her survival. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: It Would Be Night in Caracas (18)

description:
Director: Chris Atkins
Date & Time: Wednesday, June 3rd, 6.15 - 8.21 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 1
Blurb: The first-ever authorised tell-all documentary about iconic British band James.

If you ever caught yourself screaming, “Oh, sit down! Sit down! Sit down next to me!” amongst friends and strangers at a rowdy pub as a barman calls final rounds, you know you have had a proper good British night out. This feeling is courtesy of iconic Manchester band James, whose hits have accompanied generations of Brits for almost half a century. In their first-ever authorised documentary, the band chronicles their rise and fall and rise again, offering a rare insight into the thrills and woes of being a creative, as well as painting a comprehensive picture of how tabloid culture has shaped the UK music scene. A treat for fans old and new. (RAFA SALES ROSS)
name: SXSW 2026: JAMES – Getting Away With It (18)

description:
Director: Joan Porcel
Date & Time: Tuesday, June 2nd, 9.00 - 10.53 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: A queer performance artist gets dangerously close to a stranger in an online chat room.


Spanish dancer Lluis Garau adapts his own one-man-show of the same name into an electric and erotic nightmare of digital missed connections. Playing a version of himself, Garau is a young dancer with a penchant for trolling Chatroulette until someone interesting, or hot, or both, pops up. The film’s tone switches to something darker when he connects with a masked man who may be behind some targeted murders of gay men in the area. Dancing between the intimacy of dark rooms and the perceived intimacy of theaters, Garau’s central performance is so raw and all-absorbing, it becomes the film's heartbeat as the tension escalates to a fiery finale. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: La Carn (18)

description:
Director: Adrian Chiarella 
Date & Time: Friday, June 5th, 9.00 - 10.58 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 1
Blurb: A queer horror story about two teenage boys confronted with a supernatural entity who takes the form of the one they desire the most. 

The budding romance between teens Naim (Joe Bird) and Ryan (Stacy Clausen) barely has time to flourish before they are discovered and subjected to a religious conversion ritual by their local pastor. The “cure” they are subjected to unleashes a terrifying ordeal. In a cruel twist of fate, the vile, shapeshifting entity takes the form of the person they desire the most: each other. Drawing on the intimacy of modern horror as It Follows, director Adrian Chiarella announces himself as a bold new voice in genre filmmaking, delivering a visceral, emotionally charged debut that’s as devastating as it is terrifying. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: Leviticus (18)

description:
Director: John Early

Blurb: Comedian John Early’s first film is a tragicomedy about a food fluencer struggling with bulimia. 

Known for his signature offbeat delivery, comedian and actor John Early’s directorial debut delivers as much snark as it does heart. He stars as the food influencer Maddie, struggling to balance her aspirations for a career surrounded by food with a years-long eating disorder she’s kept secret from all those closes to her.  Perhaps hardest of all, Maddie’s Secret handles the horror of bulimia with startling clarity, bringing to light the shame, control and self-destruction that underpin binge-eating and purging. It’s a powerful piece of work, risky but deeply rewarding. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: Maddie's Secret (18)

description:
Director: Stephan Komandarev
Date & Time: Thursday, June 4th, 3.45 - 5.37 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 3
Blurb: A tense social drama exposing the inequality of labour in Bulgaria through a worker’s bout with COVID.

Single mother Iva (Gergana Pletnyova) feels unwell, but can’t get sick leave from her precarious clothing factory job. When she is eventually declared the first COVID case in her small Bulgarian town, a dangerous game of accusation unravels, with the labourer shunned not only from her community but also from her home. Pletnyova gives a stunning lead performance in Stephan Komandarev’s piercing social examination, a drama with the fast pulse of a thriller that closely observes a crumbling European dream from the trampoline of the pandemic. (RAFA SALES ROSS)
name: SXSW 2026: Made in EU (18)

description:
Director: Sofia Coppola 
Date & Time: Tuesday, June 2nd, 6.15 - 8.02 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 1
Blurb: Acclaimed director Sofia Coppola’s first documentary captures her long-time friend and collaborator, designer Marc Jacobs.

In her first foray into documentary-making, Oscar-winning auteur Sofia Coppola turns to her long-time friend and iconic fashion designer Marc Jacobs. Their collaborations have spanned decades, with Coppola starring in his campaigns, collaborating with him during his time at Louis Vuitton, and directing his perfume ads after he launched his own label. Taking a fly-on-the-wall approach, Coppola gives us an intimate glimpse into the everyday process and creative drive of a singular voice in the fashion world. The doc takes us back to the heyday of the nineties, when his star was on the ascendant as fashion’s most trend-averse designer, working his way from Perry Ellis to Louis Vuitton, up until the time he eventually launched his own label. Watching Marc by Sofia is an invitation to join the most glamorous of hangs. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: Marc by Sofia

description:
Director: Jennifer Ash Rudick 
Date & Time: Thursday, June 4th, 6.30 - 8.15 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: A provocative and visually arresting portrait of the artist Marilyn Minter.  

Google Marilyn Minter, and what comes up are images of women’s body parts. Mouths, eyes, feet, and tongues, covered in glitter, pearls, and dirt. Encouraged by Diane Arbus in her early years, Minter has been a working artist since the 1980s, attracting controversy for her work exploring the aesthetics of beauty, desire, and the female body. Her work has inspired the high femme aesthetic of pop culture, and she has created portraits of Jane Fonda, Pamela Anderson, Lizzo, and more. Drawing on archival material, studio footage, and (very) candid interviews, Pretty/Dirty strips away the glitter to reveal a portrait of an artist who, nearing 80, has lived a life refusing to compromise. The question remains: is the art world ready to embrace her? (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: Marilyn Minter: Pretty/Dirty (18)

description:
Director: Vladlena Sandu
Date & Time: Saturday, June 6th, 11.00 AM - 1.08 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: A haunting blend of documentary and dream, Vladlena Sandu’s film recalls growing up during wartime. 

Born in Crimea and raised in war-torn Chechnya, director Vladlena Sandu looks back at her past through fragments: family secrets, rumours, and the stories no one was allowed to tell. Using reconstructions and evocative, poetic imagery that recalls a childhood growing up in the Soviet Union, Memory is as much a personal reckoning as it is an ode to all children who grow up under siege, trying to find a language for the violence, destruction and war around them. For her feature-length debut,  Sandu collected memories from her friends, as well as her own, to create a film that is as confronting as it is healing to watch. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: Memory (18)

description:
Director: David Pablos
Date & Time: Friday, June 5th, 3.45 PM - 5.48 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: This sensual Venice winner trails a young sex worker who falls in love with an older truck driver while on the run

If Beat writer Jack Kerouac met the erotic spirit of Alfonso Cuarón’s classic Y Tu Mamá También, the result would most likely resemble David Pablos’s searing erotic drama, produced by famed Mexican actor Diego Luna. Set in the little-explored queer scene of long-distance truck driving, On the Road follows rebellious hustler Veneno (Victor Prieto Simental), who makes a meager living by trading sexual favors at a roadside diner. His life goes from stationary to transit once he meets trucker Muñeco (Osvaldo Sanchez Valenzuela), with the two rapidly embroiled in a dangerous chase as Veneno’s past threatens to catch up with them. (RAFA SALES ROSS)
name: SXSW 2026: On The Road (18)

description:
Director: Danielle Arbid
Date & Time: Monday, June 1st
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2, 6:10 PM - 8:15 PM
Blurb: A May-December romance unravels amidst a collision of desire and displacement in Beirut.


When 60-year-old Suzanne (Hiam Abass) intervenes in a racist attack against Osmane (Amine Benrachid), she feels an instant connection to the mysterious man half her age. The violence of their first meeting comes to permeate the relationship that follows, with the love between the two marked by harsh judgment of their age gap, as well as the interfaith and interracial nature of their pairing. Unable to film in Lebanon amongst the escalating war, Danielle Arbid beautifully recreates the landscape of her home city of Beirut: one constantly under attack, but still able to foster and harbour a tender love story. (ISRA AL KASSI)
name: SXSW 2026: Only Rebels Win (18)

description:
Director: Ross McElwee
Date & Time: Friday, June 5th, 1.15 PM - 3.19 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: Renowned documentarian Ross McElwee poignantly grieves the death of his son by examining a life captured on screen 

For decades, veteran American documentarian Ross McElwee captured his son’s growing up on screen, from his wife’s pregnancy in 1993’s Time Indefinite to the delicate nature of their maturing relationship in 2011’s Photographic Memory. In Remake, the director processes Adrian’s devastating death by looking back at extensive archival footage shot by two generations of McElwee men, presented alongside a stalled attempt to fictionalize his 1986 classic doc, Sherman’s March. The result is both a touching elegy and a piercing dissection of memory, image, and the blurry boundaries of personal documentary filmmaking. (RAFA SALES ROSS)
name: SXSW 2026: Remake (18)

description:
Director: Mahnaz Mohammadi
Date & Time: Friday, June 5th, 3.30 PM - 5.12 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 1
Blurb: An imprisoned teacher in Iran struggles with the weight of a forced confession. 
PLUS Q&A
The film begins in darkness, as Iranian teacher Roya (Melisa Sözen) wakes up locked in a three-by-three room. Formally daring, Mohammadi used sound and first-person point-of-view to fully immerse us in the experience of her tormented protagonist: darkness, snatches of conversation, dreams and confusion all blend. Unsure of her crime and with her family cruelly being used as blackmail, Roya is forced to choose between doing a sham confession or returning to prison. Directed by documentarian and activist Mahnaz Mohammadi, Roya is not an easy watch — but it’s a necessary one. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: Roya + Q&A (18)

description:
Director: Aitore Zholdaskali
Date & Time: Friday, June 5th, 6 PM - 8.11 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 1
Blurb: A young couple makes a dangerous fortune by faking cancer in this biting Kazakh thriller.

Set in the sprawling Kazakh metropolis of Almaty, this blood-pumping thriller follows Azamat (Ayan Utepbergen) and Tansholpan (Dilnaz Kurmangali), a young couple who are recently married and terribly broke. When violent debt collectors knock on their door, the two hatch up a plan with dangerous consequences: to fake Tansholpan’s terminal cancer to rack up donations. When the scam goes viral, the two find themselves embroiled in a dangerous web of lies that gives this fast-paced, stylish social satire about the throes of capitalism and the underbelly of social media a razor-sharp edge. (RAFA SALES ROSS)
name: SXSW 2026: SICKO (18)

description:
Director: Jane Schoenbrun 
Cast: Gillian Anderson, Hannah Einbinder 
Date & Time: Saturday 6 June, 19:00 - 21:07
Rich Mix Cinema 1
Blurb: Hannah Einbinder and Gillian Anderson star in a psychosexual horror two-hander directed by Jane Schoenbrun 

As a writer-director, Jane Schoenbrun has already built a distinctive body of work, with their horror films We’re All Going to the World’s Fair and I Saw the TV Glow exploring online life, identity, memory and nostalgia. Their latest film is a two-hander between Hannah Einbinder (Hacks) and Gillian Anderson (The X-Files, Sex Education, Scoop), with the former playing an indie filmmaker set to reboot the cult slasher flick “Camp Miasma” with the film’s original final girl, played by Anderson. Both actresses completely game to inhabit the distinctive horror-infused worlds that Schoenbrun is building. Teenage Sex and Death at Camp Miasma is an intellectual sleazefest, an ode to slashers and a takedown of modern industry politics. But at its core is the brewing connection between two women, bound by movies and desire. Every scene between Einbinder and Anderson is so charged, you’ll be afraid the screen might catch fire. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: Teenage Sex and Death at Camp Miasma

description:
Director: Sam McConnell
Date & Time: Wednesday, June 3rd, 6:30 - 8.53 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: An amateur bodybuilder wrestles with his queerness while pursuing his dreams of becoming a champion.

Eddie (Brock Yurich) dreams of becoming a bodybuilding champion and escaping rural Ohio. His gruelling training routine makes it easier to hide unspoken desires from a doting mother and girlfriend, but the pressures of an upcoming championship and the arrival of a charming coach bring his pretense to a startling halt. In a world where conversations around societal expectations on the body — both when it comes to performance and aesthetics — are everywhere, Sam McConnell offers a sharp insight into the dichotomy between a polished exterior and crumbling interior, all while sensitively observing the joys and challenges of realising one’s queerness in an environment hostile to difference. (RAFA SALES ROSS)
name: SXSW 2026: Test (18)

description:
Director: Olivia Wilde
Date & Time: Wednesday, June 3rd, 4.00 - 5.57 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 1
Blurb: Olivia Wilde directs and stars in a poignant, hilarious, and emotionally honest portrayal of human relationships. 

Annoying neighbours are a dime a dozen. Annoying neighbours who pop round to share a flan and invite you into their weekly orgy schedule – not so much. The Invite is a portrait of two married couples as they meet and clash over a neighborly get together. Joe (Seth Rogen) and Angela (Olivia Wilde), neurotic and going through a rough patch, and the intense and erotically supercharged Pina (Penélope Cruz) and Hawk (Edward Norton).  For a film set entirely in one apartment, Wilde’s direction is vivid and energetic, each scene building on and subverting the next, unpicking the layers of the relationships between the key characters. With all four cast members — Rogen, Cruz, Norton and Wilde herself — on top form, The Invite is a type of filmmaking that is sorely lacking on our screens: a film about adult feelings for adults. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: The Invite (18)

description:
Director: Tawfik Sabouni
Date & Time: Thursday, June 4th, 8.50 - 10.48 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: Five men process the trauma of captivity by reconstructing their time in a Syrian prison.

Tawfik Sabouni’s remarkable film takes its name from the infamous Syrian prison of Saidnaya, where he was kept captive during the Assad regime. Following the fall of the authoritarian ruler, the filmmaker returned to the crumbling grounds of Saidnaya alongside four other former prisoners to cathartically dissect the horror of their captivity through poetic, detailed reconstructions. In the reopening of wounds, the men powerfully reclaim their voices. As cinema tries to grapple with the horrifying effects of war and conflict as it unfolds in real time, The Other Side of the Sun proves a singular insight into the intricacies of trauma and the restorative power of art. (RAFA SALES ROSS)
name: SXSW 2026: The Other Side of the Sun (18)

description:
Director: Alex Kahuam
Date & Time:  Wednesday, June 3rd, 9:15 - 11.15 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: A son’s desperate attempt at keeping his mother alive leads him to making sacrifices in more ways than one in this stylish gothic horror.

Jason (Timothy Granaderos) is desperate to keep his mother alive by any means, so when a not-dodgy-at-all scientist (played by genre icon Doug Jones) sells him an unconventional remedy  promising to heal and extend the lifespan of anyone who takes it, Jason is in While the remedy works, it also gives  Maria an insatiable appetite for live meat – and Jason must provide. Meanwhile, his sister Rachel senses evil and turns to spells to expel it from their home. The siblings, while both desperate to keep their mother alive, clash over their approach, discovering what is truly haunting their home and their family.In the second collaboration between director Alex Kahuam and writer Daniel Kuhlman, The Remedy is an unflinching and increasingly unhinged journey between reality, nightmare and family trauma. (ISRA AL KASSI)
name: SXSW 2026: The Remedy (18)

description:
Director: Gustavo Hernández Ibáñez
Date & Time: Thursday, June 4th, 8.30 - 10.40 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 3
Blurb: Two siblings flee their violent father and come across something even more horrifying in the latest by horror master Gustavo Hernández Ibáñez. 

Vampire films are a dime a dozen. They are creatures that have been reinterpreted many times over in cinema, but when a fresh take appears, it stands out like a lighting bolt. The Whisper (El Susurro) does just that: horror master Gustavo Hernández Ibáñez (The Silent House) anchors a universal story of family, belonging and intergenerational curses in a fresh new vision of vampirism. Fleeing from their violent father, siblings Lucía (Ana Clara Guanco) and Adrián (Marcelo Michinaux) take refuge in a remote mansion, only to find that their neighbours are intending to kidnap them to produce a snuff film. As Lucía fights to protect her brother, she must face their family’s dark family curse. (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: The Whisper (18)

description:
Director: Han Chang-lok
Date & Time: Tuesday, June 2nd, 6.00 - 7.57 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 3
Blurb: A trio of teens gets into all sorts of mischief in this cool, fast-paced slice of South Korean youth

Han Chang-lok’s stylish feature debut sees a tight friendship between three teens begin to unravel following the arrival of a handsome new student at their high school. Edited with the frenzied touch of early 2000s television and featuring a soundtrack that matches that early MTV aesthetics, Funky Freaky Freaks paints a vivid portrayal of South Korean youth while drawing timely reflections on the anxieties of being young and online, the gnarly knots of Gen Z communication, and the ever-dangerous beauty standards of a generation raised on the perfectly polished imagery of pop idols. (RAFA SALES ROSS)
name: SXSW 2026: Title: Funky Freaky Freaks (18)

description:
Director: Christopher Nelius
Date & Time: Tuesday, June 2nd, 4.00 - 5.34 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 2
Blurb: “You know how to whistle, don't you?”

Not many people in the world can whistle, even fewer can whistle Mozart’s “Queen of the Night” aria. The top whistlers in the world travel from all over the world to compete in the prestigious Masters of Musical Whistling competition in L.A. 

At the heart of this riot of a documentary organiser extraordinaire and competition founder Carole Anne Kaufman. A passionate whistler, she runs a tight but gentle ship and offers a heartfelt behind the scenes look into neverending drama of event production.
Part musical, part comedy, and with the stakes of a sports movie, Whistle sets the scene for a showdown: will nerves take over? Or is it as simple as ‘put your lips together and blow’? (ISRA AL KASSI)
name: SXSW 2026: Whistle (18)

description:
Director: Kasia Adamik
Date & Time: Saturday June 6th, 4 - 6.22 PM
RichMix Screen: Cinema 1
Blurb: Lesley Manville leads a tense Cold War thriller based on a story by Nobel Prize-winning author Olga Tokarzuk. 

Tightly wound British academic Joan, played by the always excellent Lesley Manville, travels to Cold War-era Warsaw to deliver a paper on the treatment of psychiatric illness. Once in Poland, the professor is surprisingly caught in between a student uprising and escalating assassinations as authorities call in martial law.Manville is phenomenal as a spiky, on-edge professor who’s pulled into something much bigger than herself and, as Joan’s stern armour unravels, she (and we) are coolly reminded of the narrowness of her experience after being dropped into a brutal world where theory isn’t going to cut it.  (ANNA BOGUTSKAYA)
name: SXSW 2026: Winter of the Crow (18)

description:
Director: Ali Mosaffa
Cast: Ali Mosaffa, Zuzana Stivínová, Magdalena Borová, Leila Hatami

An Iranian man investigating his father’s mysterious past in Prague finds himself drawn into the life of another, a near-dead stranger who shares his heritage. Rouzbeh (Ali Mosaffa), far from his troubled family in Tehran, uncovers unexpected connections while retracing his father’s steps and confronting the echoes of absence and identity left behind.

Directed by acclaimed filmmaker Ali Mosaffa (Portrait of a Lady Far Away, The Last Step), Absence is a mystical thriller exploring memory, identity, and the invisible threads that bind past and present.
name: VAAK Festival 2025: ABSENCE (18)

description:
Directors: Raha Amirfazli, Alireza Ghasemi
Cast: Hamideh Jafari, Bashir Nikzad, Mohammad Hosseini

Three members of an extended Afghan family start their lives over in Iran as refugees, unaware that they face a decades-long struggle to truly feel “at home.” The story follows their resilience, hopes, and the challenges they encounter while trying to carve out a sense of belonging in a foreign land.

The film premiered at the Sundance Film Festival 2024, where it won the Directing Award (World Cinema – Dramatic) and was nominated for the Grand Jury Prize. It also won Best Film at Film Fest Sundsvall 2024, Grand Prize & Best Actress (Hamideh Jafari) at Saint-Jean-de-Luz International Film Festival 2024, and Best Director at the Malaysia International Film Festival 2024. It was further nominated for the Golden Frog at Camerimage 2024 (Directors’ Debuts Competition).
name: VAAK Festival 2025: In the Land of Brothers (18)

description:
Director: Saeed Roostaei
Cast: Parinaz Izadyar, Peyman Maadi, Soha Niasti, Hassan Pourshirazi

A devoted mother’s life unravels when tragedy strikes, forcing her to confront loss, responsibility, and the pursuit of justice. Mahnaz (lead actress) is a 50-year-old widowed nurse, raising her children alone while struggling with her rebellious son Aliyar. As she prepares to marry her fiancé Hamid, Aliyar’s suspension from school triggers a chain of events that turns her world upside down.

Premiering in the main competition at the 78th Cannes Film Festival on 22 May 2025 and nominated for the Palme d’Or, the film was theatrically released in Iran on 23 July 2025.
